summary: Alfred is a local-only text editor binary with no network exposure, no authentication layer, and no secrets in source. The security surface is narrow: file-system access via Lisp primitives and plugin loading from the local 'plugins/' directory. The primary concerns are unrestricted path traversal in Lisp filesystem primitives (list-dir, open-file, save-buffer, path-join) and Lisp code execution from arbitrary plugin files, with no sandboxing. No hardcoded credentials or injection vulnerabilities were detected.

owasp_findings.description: The (list-dir path) Lisp primitive accepts any absolute or relative path string with no restrictions. A malicious or compromised plugin can enumerate any directory on the system the process has read access to.
owasp_findings.evidence: define_native_closure(&env, "list-dir", move |_env, args| { let path_str = extract_string_arg(&args, "list-dir")?; let path = std::path::Path::new(&path_str); let read_dir = match std::fs::read_dir(path) {
owasp_findings.remediation: Consider restricting accessible paths to a configurable allowlist or the user's home/project directory. At minimum, document the intentional unrestricted access.

owasp_findings.description: The (open-file path) primitive opens any file path without validation. Plugins can silently read arbitrary files on disk.
owasp_findings.evidence: define_native_closure(&env, "open-file", move |_env, args| { let path_str = extract_string_arg(&args, "open-file")?; let path = std::path::Path::new(&path_str); match alfred_core::buffer::Buffer::from_file(path) {
owasp_findings.remediation: Add a path validation function that rejects paths outside expected working directories, or add a user-confirmation prompt for paths outside the CWD.

owasp_findings.description: The (save-buffer path) primitive writes to any writable path without restriction. A malicious plugin could overwrite system files or write outside the project directory.
owasp_findings.remediation: Validate the target write path. Require confirmation or restrict to CWD subtree.

owasp_findings.description: Plugins are loaded from the 'plugins/' directory relative to the current working directory. Any directory named 'plugins/' in the cwd will have its Lisp files evaluated with full editor state access. There is no plugin signature verification or integrity check.
owasp_findings.evidence: let plugins_dir = Path::new("plugins"); let (discovered, discovery_errors) = discovery::scan(plugins_dir);
owasp_findings.remediation: Document the trust model explicitly. Consider pinning or checksumming plugin files. Avoid loading plugins from world-writable directories.

owasp_findings.description: The user configuration file (~/.config/alfred/init.lisp) is evaluated at startup with full bridge primitive access. There is no sandboxing of the config file evaluation.
owasp_findings.evidence: let home_dir = std::env::var("HOME").unwrap_or_default(); ... let config_path = config_file_path(&home_dir); if let Some(error_msg) = load_user_config(&runtime, &config_path)
owasp_findings.remediation: This is expected behavior for an editor (analogous to .vimrc). Document the trust model. The current behavior is appropriate for a local config.

owasp_findings.description: The :e (open file) colon command accepts any file path provided by the user without additional path validation beyond what the OS provides.
owasp_findings.evidence: cmd if cmd.starts_with("e ") => { let path = cmd.strip_prefix("e ").unwrap().trim().to_string(); ColonCommand::OpenFile(path) }
owasp_findings.remediation: Low risk for a local editor tool. No action required unless used in an environment with shared accounts.

unvalidated_entries.description: (list-dir path): path string accepted with no sanitization or scope restriction
(open-file path): path string accepted with no sanitization or scope restriction

patterns_found: No authentication layer (local desktop application -- not applicable)

details: No known CVEs identified from dependency names and versions: ropey 1.x, thiserror 1.x, rust_lisp 0.18.0, tree-sitter 0.26, crossterm 0.28, ratatui 0.29. Run 'cargo audit' for authoritative CVE scanning.

recommendations.title: Document and optionally scope Lisp filesystem primitive access
recommendations.description: The list-dir, open-file, and save-buffer Lisp primitives allow plugins to read and write any path accessible to the process. For a local editor this is an expected capability, but it should be explicitly documented as a trust boundary. Optionally, add a configurable path allowlist or a 'safe-paths' mechanism for shared-machine deployments.

recommendations.title: Add plugin integrity verification
recommendations.description: Plugins are loaded by scanning the 'plugins/' directory relative to CWD. Consider adding checksum/hash verification for distributed plugins, and documenting that the plugins/ directory is a trusted execution boundary.

recommendations.title: Run cargo audit in CI pipeline
recommendations.description: Add 'cargo audit' to the CI workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml) to automatically detect dependencies with known CVEs on each push.
